ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "middle_name" TEXT /* Middle name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "suffix" TEXT /* Suffix of the customer's name */,
  "address_line1" TEXT /* First line of the customer's address */,
  "address_line2" TEXT /* Second line of the customer's address */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the customer resides */,
  "state_province_name" TEXT /* State or province where the customer resides */,
  "country_region_name" TEXT /* Country or region where the customer resides */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the customer's address */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the customer */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "home_owner_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the customer is a homeowner */,
  "number_cars_owned" INTEGER /* Number of cars owned by the customer */,
  "number_children_at_home" INTEGER /* Number of children living at the customer's home */,
  "total_children" INTEGER /* Total number of children the customer has */,
  "yearly_income" INTEGER /* Yearly income of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language for communication */,
  "customer_since" TEXT /* Date the customer first interacted with the company */,
  "last_purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the customer's last purchase */,
  "total_spent" REAL /* Total amount spent by the customer */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Loyalty points accumulated by the customer */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer segment (optional, as we have customer_segment_mapping) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer involved in the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., phone call, email, purchase) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date when the interaction occurred */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interaction */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel of interaction (e.g., phone, email, in-store) */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who handled the interaction */,
  "interaction_rating" INTEGER /* Customer's rating of the interaction (e.g., on a scale of 1-5) */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Resolution status of the interaction (e.g., resolved, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_purchases" (
  "purchase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the purchase */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product purchased */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product purchased */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date when the purchase was made */,
  "total_price" REAL /* Total price of the purchase */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used (e.g., credit card, cash, PayPal) */,
  "order_status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "delivery_date" TEXT /* Expected or actual delivery date */,
  "shipping_address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shipping address (if different from the billing address) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_purchases_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_purchases_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "brand" TEXT /* Brand of the product */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the product */,
  "stock_quantity" INTEGER /* Available stock quantity */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer preference */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who has the preference */,
  "preference_type" TEXT /* Type of preference (e.g., email, SMS, product category) */,
  "preference_details" TEXT /* Details of the preference */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the preference was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the preference (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_preferences_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the segment was created */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date the segment was last modified */,
  "segment_size" INTEGER /* Number of customers in the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segment_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment the customer belongs to */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the customer joined the segment */,
  "exit_date" TEXT /* Date the customer left the segment (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segment_mapping_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segment_mapping_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer segment targeted by the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "campaign_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager responsible for the campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the campaign */,
  "campaign_status" TEXT /* Status of the campaign (e.g., planned, active, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marketing_campaigns_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing campaign */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., reach, engagement, conversion) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric (e.g., internal, external) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_performance_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES marketing_campaigns ("campaign_id")
)
